title: R-XX Requirement Row
description: A single row from a 6-NUX REQUIREMENTS.md table. Column order: # | Requirement | Status | Notes. The '#' column holds the R-ID; 'Requirement' is the discriminator column that parsers use to identify requirement tables (other tables use different column 2 headers such as 'Domain').

id.description: R-ID in canonical form. 1-3 digits, optional single lowercase alpha suffix.

requirement.description: Human-readable requirement description. Free text; must be non-empty.

status.description: Canonical status after normalization. Must be one of the 8 known statuses (case-sensitive, uppercase). 'BLOCKED-EXTERNAL' is distinct from 'BLOCKED' — use BLOCKED-EXTERNAL when the blocker is a vendor, regulator, or third party outside engineering control.

notes.description: Free-text notes. May be empty string or absent. Escaped pipes (\|) in source markdown are resolved to '|' before validation.

examples.requirement: 8-module EPOD form (Classification, Legal, Financial, Dates, Ownership, Compliance, Validity, Submission)
examples.notes: All 8 modules functional, 34 QA defects fixed

examples.requirement: Sub-item introduced after R-98 was already assigned
EventEmitter bus for async claim lifecycle events
examples.notes: In-process bus works; AWS SNS/SQS/EventBridge is Tier 2B AWS Sprint dependency
